text:
## Team Context

**Your assigned name**: spec-developer-backend-notes
**Team Lead**: team-lead (use SendMessage to reach it)

**Communication Protocol** (you have SendMessage in your tools):
- `QUESTION: ...` — before starting, if requirements have genuine ambiguity
- `BLOCKER: ...` — immediately if you cannot proceed
- `DONE: {summary}` — when your deliverables are complete
- `SUGGESTION: ...` — proactively flag tech issues, arch concerns, refactoring ideas

**Context Strategy**: repomix
All relevant context is pre-loaded. Use Read/Glob/Grep for additional files.

**Escalation rule**: Do NOT work silently on ambiguity. Ask first.
**Team culture**: Your domain expertise is valued. Speak up.

## Tasks (4 tasks, do them all sequentially)

### Task 1: notesaner-8jw — Note Content Indexing Pipeline for FTS
BullMQ job pipeline to index note content into PostgreSQL full-text search.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- BullMQ job: IndexNoteJob triggered on note save (debounced)
- Read MD file content, strip frontmatter
- Extract plain text from Markdown
- Build tsvector with weights: title (A), headings (B), content (C), tags (D)
- Update notes.search_vector
- Index frontmatter properties
- Batch reindex endpoint: POST /admin/search/reindex

### Task 2: notesaner-57r — Attachment Upload and Management
File attachment system for images and documents embedded in notes.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- POST /notes/:id/attachments — multipart upload endpoint
- Supported types: images (jpg, png, gif, webp, svg), PDF, common docs
- File size limit: 50MB per file (configurable)
- Files stored in workspace attachments directory
- GET /attachments/:id endpoint to serve files with correct MIME type
- DELETE /attachments/:id endpoint
- Attachment record in DB with noteId, filename, mimeType, size, path
- Orphan attachment cleanup job (BullMQ)

### Task 3: notesaner-bnd — Comment/Annotation System API
Inline comment threads for collaborative review on notes.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- POST /notes/:id/comments — create comment with position JSON
- GET /notes/:id/comments — list all comments (including threads)
- PATCH /comments/:id — edit comment content
- DELETE /comments/:id — delete comment
- POST /comments/:id/replies — add reply to thread
- PATCH /comments/:id/resolve — mark thread as resolved
- @mention support: parse @username and notify mentioned user
- Comments sorted by position (top to bottom)

### Task 4: notesaner-afv3 — Note Content Hash Validation
Detect external changes to note files via content hashing.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- SHA-256 hash of file content stored as notes.contentHash
- On read: compare file hash with stored hash
- If different: external change detected, re-index and notify via WebSocket
- File watcher also updates hash on change
- Atomic hash update: update hash and content together
- API response includes contentHash for client-side change detection

## Project Info
- NestJS 11, Prisma 6, PostgreSQL 17, BullMQ
- Project root: /Users/zeizel/projects/notesaner
- Notes module: apps/server/src/modules/notes/
- Jobs module: apps/server/src/modules/jobs/
- Search module: apps/server/src/modules/search/
- Files service: apps/server/src/modules/files/files.service.ts
- DTOs in apps/server/src/modules/notes/dto/
- Tests go in apps/server/src/modules/notes/__tests__/ or __tests__/ in respective module

## Key Architecture Decisions
- BullMQ queues already configured (jobs.module.ts, jobs.service.ts)
- note-indexing queue exists with NoteIndexingProcessor
- FilesService handles all filesystem I/O with path traversal prevention
- Use existing SearchService.indexNote() for the FTS update
- Comment model exists in Prisma schema
- contentHash field exists on Note model

Write unit tests for all new services.
summary: FTS indexing pipeline + attachments + comments + content hash
